title: andropogon diuturnus
intro: Andropogon diuturnus, also known as the Durva grass or Saccharum grass, is a type of grass species native to India and parts of Asia. It is considered a sacred plant in Hinduism, known for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Andropogon diuturnus include reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and alleviating respiratory problems. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often used in traditional Ayurvedic and Unani medicine.
